note: Narrow CME first seen to the E in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-01-01T03:53Z. This CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2 for four frames. The later propagation of this CME is missed due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery starting after 2026-01-01T03:48Z There are a couple of potential sources for this CME. The first is an eruption and some associated dimming from AR 14324 best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-01-01T01:39Z. The second is associated with a C9.6 class flare from AR 14325 (S10E03) that peaked at 2026-01-01T02:25Z. The flare and associated eruption are best seen in SDO AIA 131. Based on the latitude of the CME, the second source candidate is more likely.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen because this CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-01T06:24Z. This CME is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is a large filament eruption over the SE quadrant of the Earth-facing disk. The eruption is best seen in SDO AIA 304, and the filament can be seen rising as early as 2026-01-01T04:52Z. There is some brightening on the SE limb in running difference imagery as early as 2026-01-01-T05:36Z (likely associated with brightening from the rising filament itself), but the structure of the CME is not visible in white light imagery until 2026-01-01T06:24Z. There appears to be some deflection slightly to the south later into the propagation of the CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Best-fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and on the visible location of the filament. There is also a wider, more northern portion that is not visible by the time the CME makes it into SOHO LASCO C3. Because the extra feature is not visible later in SOHO LASCO C3, the bright bulk has been prioritized for the measurement. The extra, wider feature is more prominent in GOES CCOR-1, but it is still not as bright as the main bulk. The approximate longitude of the filament footpoints is around E60 to E80. Since a triangulated measurement cannot be made as there is no STEREO A COR2 imagery for this event, a starting point of E60 was used for this measurement. Because of this, there will inherently be a greater than normal degree of uncertainty associated with this measurement.

note: CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-01T13:36Z. The CME is also seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1, but is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting after 2026-01-01T04:38Z. There is no good source candidate for this feature visible on the Earth-facing disk. This is likely just outflow following CME: 2026-01-01T06:24Z. There is some southward deflection later into the propagation past the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of distinct source candidate and lack of available coronagraph triangulation.

note: CME first seen to the NNE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-01T19:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is currently not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap which began at 2026-01-01T04:38Z. The source of this event is a C6.2 flare from AR 14324 (N24E01) seen in SDO AIA 131 peaking at 2026-01-01T17:59Z. A wide region of dimming and field line opening is also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284, followed by post eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 131 and 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Faint loop CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-01T23:12Z as well as by GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 (after the data gap) in later frames. The source of this event is faint field line movement seen along the NW limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-01-01T22:36Z. The source could be Active Region 14317, which is in the process of rotation off the visible Earth-facing disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source is behind the NW limb in SDO and this event is covered by an unusually long data gap in STEREO A imagery.

note: Faint CME seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 and CCOR-1. It is partially obscured by pylon in C3. The CME has a somewhat brighter narrow bulk and a very faint very wide (partial halo-like) shock. No clear source was found in EUV imagery but there is some minor movement of field lines well beyond the limb as seen in the W in GOES SUVI 284 after 2026-01-02T16:17Z. Some ejecta can also be seen from beyond the W limb in STA EUVI 304 around 2026-01-02T16:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis of the faint wide shock of this CME with one-coronagraph method (SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs), based on the fit of the partial halo front with coronagraphs. The source is far-sided and the exact longitude is not known.

Preliminary BULK analysis, as a plane-of-sky with SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 only due to the data gap in STEREO A COR2 and STEREO A EUVI starting around 2026-01-02T16:53Z imagery to see and triangulate the far-sided source.

note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-01-02T16:53Z. The source is likely faintly observed on the NE disk as a slow filament eruption/surface brightening from two nearby areas, which likely created this CME and the following similar CME: 2026-01-02T21:12Z, with difficulty distinguishing the sources due to their proximity and slow nature. The first area observed as surface brightening in SDO AIA 304 is centered around N40E22 and starts erupting around 2026-01-02T17:30Z. The second area observed as a filament, which spanned approximately N50E20 to N45E10, slowly peeling off the disk in SDO AIA 304 starting as early as 2026-01-02T17:52Z. In SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284, both of these eruptions are observed deflecting field lines further to the NW from these areas, with some dimming in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky as no STEREO A COR2 imagery is available to triangulate this CME, which is observed deflected NW away from its source on the NE earth-facing disk.

note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-01-02T16:53Z. The source is likely faintly observed on the NE disk as a slow filament eruption/surface brightening from two nearby areas, which likely created this CME and the preceeding similar CME: 2026-01-02T19:00Z, with difficulty distinguishing the sources due to their proximity and slow nature. The first area observed as surface brightening in SDO AIA 304 is centered around N40E22 and starts erupting around 2026-01-02T17:30Z. The second area observed as a filament, which spanned approximately N50E20 to N45E10, slowly peeling off the disk in SDO AIA 304 starting as early as 2026-01-02T17:52Z. In SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284, both of these eruptions are observed deflecting field lines further to the NW from these areas, with some dimming in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky as no STEREO A COR2 imagery is available to triangulate this CME, which is observed deflected NW away from its source on the NE earth-facing disk.

note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely related to an eruption from Active Region 14334 (S18E70) starting around 2026-01-03T07:20Z seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. An opening of field lines can be observed over the southeast limb in GOES SUVI 284 imagery at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the south/southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to a filament eruption hovering near S30W65 which begins to lift off around 2026-01-03T08:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: Jet-like CME seen to the southeast in SOHO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2A associated with a point source eruption near the vicinity of AR 14334 (S15W59) seen best in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-01-04T00:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using SOHO and STEREO A for triangulation of the longitude.

note: Outflow-like CME seen to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 likely associated with far-sided eruptive activity suggested by the geometry of the CME as observed from STEREO A. May be associated with some rising field lines observed behind the northwest limb as seen in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-01-04T04:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Low confidence measurement as the front of the CME is not easily seen in available coronagraph imagery.

note: Faint outflow-like CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO and not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap; a possible source for this coronal mass ejection is eruptive activity just behind the eastern limb with a rising prominence seen best in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-01-04T10:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: One spacecraft plane of sky measurement as the CME is not seen well in STEREO A COR2A imagery.

note: Narrow CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and later in STEREO A COR2A which could be associated with a sympathetic eruption near the eastern limb caused by the prominence behind the eastern limb beginning near 2026-01-04T12:00Z seen best in SDO AIA 171.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation of the CME leading edge to determine longitude.

note: Very faint CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-04T21:12Z. This CME fades before exiting the field of view in C2 and is therefore not seen in any other coronagraphs. The source of this event is likely some minor field line movement seen along the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA starting around 2026-01-04T21:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to lack of visible coronagraph imagery and source near the limb.

note: Narrow, bright CME seen to the south-southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and to the southeast in STEREO A COR2A. The likely source is a thin streamer of plasma lifting from near AR 4334 (S17E43) with noticeable southern deflection seen best in SDO AIA 304 and SDO AIA 171 around 2026-01-05T07:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft out to 12 solar radii which suggests strong southern deflection.

note: Faint CME seen to the southeast in SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2A and GOES CCOR-1 possibly associated with a two-part eruption in AR 4334 (S17E43) seen best in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-01-05T08:20Z; while most of the plasma material is directed southward, a portion of the plasma lags behind and is directed to the southeast. May instead be associated with a destabilizing magnetic loop with bright post eruptive arcades high above the AR 4334 (S17E43), but the ejecta does not leave the GOES and SDO EUV field of views until after 09:00Z, suggesting that any such CME associated with the later eruption in AR 4334 is seen after 09:00Z in coronagraph imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A SECCHI COR2A, and GOES CCOR-1 out to about 12 solar radii.

note: CME seen to the southeast in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 but not in SOHO LASCO due to a data gap; the CME is probably associated with destabilizing magnetic loop with bright post eruptive arcades near AR 4334 (S17E43) seen best in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-01-05T08:20Z seen through 2026-01-05T09:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Low confidence leading edge measurement using triangulation between GOES and STEREO A to determine the originating source longitude.

note: Wide CME first seen to the East in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-06T02:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-01-05T17:23Z to 2026-01-06T04:23Z. The source of this event is a C2.2 class flare and associated eruption from Active Region 14334 (S15E33) which began at approx. 2026-01-06T00:15Z. This flare is visible in SDO AIA 131, along with dimming seen in SDO AIA 193 and 211. Field line opening can be seen in SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Very faint CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-06T07:36Z. This event is too faint to be clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. The likely source of this event is some general field line movement near Active Region 14334 (S15E30) seen in SDO AIA 171.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

note: Faint CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-06T12:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this event is likely field line movement seen along the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-01-06T11:42Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to source near the limb and lack of STEREO A for triangulation.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-06T14:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This CME is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a minor filament eruption seen from near AR 14334 (S15E27) beginning at approx. 2026-01-06T13:31Z as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME visible to the NE in STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely a C4.0 flare starting at 2026-01-06T15:43Z from Active Region 14334 (S13E25) as seen in SDO AIA 131. An eruption with dimming and post-eruptive arcades is best seen in SDO AIA 193. This CME likely exhibits deflection within the field of view. A smaller eruption is seen from Active Region 14336 (approximately S09E57) starting around 2026-01-06T15:07Z, but the larger eruption is believed to be the most likely source candidate.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location. Only one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for this measurement, so the fit is approximate.

note: CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-01-06T18:23Z. The source is likely far-sided, with no clear source on the disk seen in EUV imagery. Field line movement may be seen beyond the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA starting around 2026-01-06T21:06Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

note: CME first seen to the ESE in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-01-07T04:23Z. The exact start time is somewhat unclear due to imagery quality. This CME is also seen in to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The leading edge of the CME is extremely faint and difficult to see in white light imagery. The source of this CME is likely associated with one of a series of eruptions from AR 14334. The eruption beginning around 2026-01-07T03:00Z associated with a C2.9 flare from AR 14334 (S15E18) that peaked at 2026-01-07T03:54Z is the most likely source candidate. The eruptive behavior, including some filamentary structure, and flare can be see across SDO wavelengths but are best seen in SDO AIA 131 and SDO AIA 304. There appears to be some deflection possibly due to the southern extension of coronal hole W of AR 14334 that was centered approximately around S18E05 at the time of the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as the visible on-disk source location.

note: CME visible to the SE in STEREO A COR2 and the SSE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The CME may be partially obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The likely source is an eruption with dimming seen south of AR 14334, around S30E25, starting around 2026-01-07T03:52Z as best seen in SDO AIA 193. The eruption is also seen from the point of view of STEREO A 195, closer to the southeast limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the E in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-01-07T06:53Z. This CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2. The source is most likely an eruption from AR 14334 (S15E16) associated with a C7.4 flare that peaked at 2026-01-07T06:12Z. The flare is best seen in SDO AIA 131, and the associated eruption is best seen in SDO AIA 193 and SDO AIA 304. There appears to be some deflection possibly due to the southern extension of coronal hole W of AR 14334 that was centered approximately around S18E03 at the time of the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: This measurement is somewhat subjective because this CME is only visible in STEREO A COR2. The longitude in the measurement is based on the source location on the Earth-facing disk.

note: CME first seen to the SE in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-01-07T10:38Z. This CME is also seen to the SSE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. It is partially obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is most likely continuous eruptive activity from AR 14334 (S15E14) that persists for several hours starting as early as 2026-01-07T09:00Z. The ejecta that is most likely associated with this CME is best seen leaving the solar disk in SDO AIA 193 around 2026-01-07T12:07Z. There is some deflection likely due to the proximity to the southern extension of coronal hole W of AR 14334 that was centered approximately around S18E00 at the time of the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source on the Earth-facing disk.

note: This CME is visible to the north/northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best f it between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption observed over/near the northwest limb starting around 2026-01-08T02:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. An opening of field lines can also be observed over the northwest limb at this time in SDO AIA 131/171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is more narrow as observed by STEREO A COR2 imagery, while a wider feature is observed in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is an eruption and C4.4 flare from Active Region 14334 (S18E05) starting around 2026-01-08T05:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. Ejecta can be observed deflecting southeast of the eruption site, possibly due to a nearby coronal hole west of AR 14334.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement following the wider feature seen in SOHO LASCO imagery. The measured parameters were obtained between the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible as a halo in GOES CCOR-1 imagery and a partial halo directed to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in real-time due to a planned gap between downlink periods starting at 2026-01-08T09:48Z. The source of this event is far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event. However, it is possible this event is associated with former Active Region 14321, which is would have been located roughly around S02E169 at the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk measurement tracking the inner, brighter feature based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
SHOCK measurement following the fainter, outer feature. The measured parameters were obtained from the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the E/SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. It is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: This CME measurement includes at least three separate fronts visible in SOHO LASCO C2 frames that morph into what appears to be one single CME within the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view. Therefore it was measured as one CME.

note: This CME is only visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery to the west. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky fit using STEREO A COR2 (longitude of 141 based on STEREO A COR2's POS at start time of CME).

note: Faint CME seen (mostly in running difference imagery) to the E in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1, covered by the data gap in STEREO A. The source is likely an eruption N of AR 14336 (S10E33), seen as dark moving ejecta in SDO AIA 304 starting at 2026-01-08T14:48Z and centered initially ~(N03E30). There could be eastward deflection of this CME because of a small coronal hole just west of its source.

cmeAnalyses.note: One-coronagraph analysis (SOHO LASCO) based on the location of the source eruption.

note: Faint CME to the E in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1, closely following CME: 2026-01-08T15:48Z. There is a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source for it has been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph because of a daily data gap and no source has been found for it in EUV imagery. Only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

note: Bright partial halo CME to the S and SE in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs, fnot seen in STEREO A COR2 due to the daily data gap. Its source is a massive, very complex filament eruption S and E of Active Region 14334 (S19E03), potentially involving two erupting filaments (one directly south of this active region and another more to the east. This eruption starts after ~2026-01-08T16Z to the SE of disk center and is associated with the long-duration C5.6 flare in this active region peaking at 2026-01-08T17:30Z. It is seen in SDO AIA 304/193 as bright, extensive post-eruptive arcades, a large-area dimming, faint EUV wave and erupting filaments seen in 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Analysis of the fainter leading shock front with one coronagraph method (SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1) based on the shape of the front; this analysis fits the location of the source - flare and eruption near AR 14334.
Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis of the southern lobe of this two-lobed event based on the location of the source. The source is a long filament, so analysis is very approximate.
Approximate, one-coronagraph (early SOHO imagery and GOES CCOR-1 imagery) analysis of this CME based on the location of the source location (south of AR 14334).

note: Partial halo CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 at the same time as the more southern CME: 2026-01-08T17:12Z. Alternatively, these two events could be two lobes of the same CME. This event is fully obscured by the daily data gap in STEREO A. The source could be the more eastern part of the complex U-shaped filament eruption to the SE of Active Region 14334 (S19E03) (or potentially one of two erupting filaments). This complex eruption starts after ~2026-01-08T16Z to the SE of disk center. It is seen in SDO AIA 304/193 as bright, extensive post-eruptive arcades near the active region, a large-area dimming, and faint EUV wave. Erupting filaments are seen in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis of the eastern lobe of this complex eruption.

note: Very faint possible CME front visible only in difference imagery to the N/NW in SOHO LASCO C2. The source is believed to be a C3.4 class flare from AR 13443 (S14W04) with a very clear dimming structure and associated EUV wave heavily deflected to the N/NW as seen in SDO/AIA 193. This deflection may be due to coronal holes nearby.

cmeAnalyses.note: HIGHLY SPECULATIVE ANALYSIS. Very faint front measured to the N/NW starting with the potential source location and adjusting to fit the front. Deflection highly likely with this event based on dimming seen on solar disk.

note: CME observed to the SE in STEREO A COR2, also observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C3 following a data gap ending at 2026-01-09T04:22Z and in GOES CCOR-1 heavily obscured by the pylon. No distinct source for this CME is observed, but is likely outflow following large CME 2026-01-08T17:00Z from Active Region 14334 (S19E03) and a large dimming region to its south. SWPC_CAT triangulation corroborates this on-disk source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3.

note: CME observed to the SE in STEREO A COR2, also observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 following a data gap ending for LASCO C2 at 2026-01-09T04:17Z and in GOES CCOR-1 heavily obscured by the pylon. No distinct source for this CME is observed, but is likely outflow following large CME 2026-01-08T17:00Z from Active Region 14334 (S19E03) and a large dimming region to its south. SWPC_CAT triangulation corroborates this on-disk source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: This narrow CME is faintly visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. It is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME, but based on lack of visibility in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 it is likely a far-sided event from Earth's perspective.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. The longitude for STEREO A's plane-of-sky is roughly 141 degrees at the start time of the CME.

note: CME observed to the SE in STEREO A COR2, also observed to the S in LASCO C3 following a data gap ending at 2026-01-09T12:30Z and in GOES CCOR-1 heavily obscured by the pylon. The source is likely outflow following large CME 2026-01-08T17:00Z from Active Region 14334 (S19E03) and a large dimming region to its south, with some extra dimming observed centered around S60E20 in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-01-09T06:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only measurement due to SOHO data gap and GOES CCOR-1 pylon obstruction of the CME, using the observed source centered around S60E20 as a basis. As such the latitude and longitude could easily vary +-5 degrees either direction, but high confidence in being out of the ecliptic.

note: This CME is visible to the south in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and southeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 as it is blocked by the pylon. The source may be related to faint dimming centered near S50E20 starting around 2026-01-09T14:30Z seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: A very faint narrow jet-like CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 difference imagery and possibly seen very faintly in one or two timestamps of STEREO A COR2 after the data gap (but very faintly). Too faint to discern the front in GOES CCOR-1 and C3. Source could be a minor movement of field line on SE limb seen in SDO AIA 171 seen ~2026-01-10T01:50Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative analysis since the only possible (and extremely minor) source seems to be on/close/behind the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 but (a very uncertain) fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and one or two rather questionable timestamps of STEREO A COR2) produces a fit of ~70 deg of longitude.

note: Very slow, streamer blowout CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-10T16:12Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. There is no definitive source for this CME on the Earth-facing disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. SOHO LASCO C2 was not used as the CME moves more slowly in early frames.

note: Thin jet-like CME observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and unseen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-01-10T14:53Z. The source is a complex eruption associated with a C2.1 flare (S16W04) from AR 14339 peaking at 2026-01-10T19:42Z observed in SDO AIA 131, observed as what is likely two bulk fronts as CME: 2026-01-10T20:36Z and CME: 2026-01-10T20:48Z. Following the flare, filament ejecta is observed in SDO AIA 304 traveling S/SW but appearing to mostly fall back onto the disk likely associated with this CME, and that is followed by dimming starting around 2026-01-10T19:57Z centered around S27W02 observed in SDO AIA 193 likely associated with CME: 2026-01-10T20:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Half width less than 10 degrees. Single spacecraft 3D analysis due to lack of COR2A imagery and CCOR-1 pylon obstruction, using the observed flare source S16W04 as a basis. However, observed deflection of the CME in SDO AIA imagery and the inability to triangulate with other coronagraphs makes this analysis speculative, and the latitude/longitude could easily range within +-10 degrees.

note: Faint CME observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and unseen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-01-10T14:53Z. The source is a complex eruption associated with a C2.1 flare (S16W04) from AR 14339 peaking at 2026-01-10T19:42Z observed in SDO AIA 131, observed as what is likely two bulk fronts as CME: 2026-01-10T20:36Z and CME: 2026-01-10T20:48Z. Following the flare, filament ejecta is observed in SDO AIA 304 traveling S/SW but appearing to mostly fall back onto the disk likely associated with CME: 2026-01-10T20:36Z, and that is followed by dimming starting around 2026-01-10T19:57Z centered around S27W02 observed in SDO AIA 193 likely associated with this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft 3D analysis due to lack of COR2A imagery and CCOR-1 pylon obstruction, using the observed dimming source S27W02 as a basis. However, observed deflection of the CME in SDO AIA imagery and the inability to triangulate with other coronagraphs makes this analysis speculative, and the latitude/longitude/half-width could easily range within +-5 degrees.

note: Faint CME observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-01-10T14:53Z. The CME front appears to form within the LASCO C2 field-of-view rather than appear behind the occulting disk, as such the start time is this first visible frame. No distinct on-disk source is observed.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C3 only plane-of-sky due to unobserved source and no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate.

note: Faint CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-11T01:25Z. This CME is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2. There is no obvious source for this CME on the Earth-facing disk, so it is most likely far-sided. The is some minor field line movement near/beyond the SE limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting as early as 2026-01-11T00:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement (Lon. -90) based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Narrow, jet-like CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-01-11T05:12Z. This CME is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASOC C3, GOES CCOR-1, and very faintly in STEREO A COR2. There is no definitive source for this CME on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. There is slow, broad field line movement, beyond the E limb for several hours prior to the start of this CME best seen in GOES SUVI 284, but it is most likely unrelated.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1 and in running difference imagery in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 in real-time due to a data gap. The potential source may be an eruption with dimming near S10E50 starting around 2026-01-11T16:12Z, best seen in SDO AIA 193. This CME is quickly overtaken in the field of view by CME with ID 2026-01-11T22:45:00-CME-001.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location. Limited imagery was used for this measurement due to the CME's faintness.

note: CME seen to the east in GOES CCOR-1 and to the east in running difference imagery in real-time for SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source is an M3.3 flare as seen on the east limb across all wavelengths of SDO/AIA imagery starting at 2026-01-11T21:53Z. Rapidly opening field lines and post-eruptive arcades are also visible. The potential source for this flare and CME may be AR 14321, possibly located around S10E115, which rotated off of the Earth-facing disk on 2025-12-31, and may be a region of interest seen in NSO/GONG data.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on potential source location using GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Shock measurement based on the potential source location, with adjustment to best fit the shock of the CME in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.

note: Faint, fast CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, overlapping heavily with CME 2026-01-11T22:45Z. Not visible in STEREO A COR2, either due to faintness of front or being directed behind the occulting disk. No discernable source is observed for this CME, but is likely outflow with continued field line movement in SDO AIA 171 following significant O-type CME 2026-01-11T22:45Z, which had a potential source of former AR 14321, possibly located around S10E115, which rotated off of the Earth-facing disk on 2025-12-31, and may be a region of interest seen in NSO/GONG data.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, using E115 as the source longitude as this CME is likely outflow from CME: 2026-01-11T22:45Z with E115 as its bulk. Longitude could easily vary +-10 degrees.

note: Faint, fast CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, overlapping heavily with CME 2026-01-11T22:45Z. Not visible in STEREO A COR2, either due to faintness of front or being directed behind the occulting disk. No discernable source is observed for this CME, but is likely outflow with continued field line movement in SDO AIA 171 following significant O-type CME 2026-01-11T22:45Z, which had a potential source of former AR 14321, possibly located around S10E115, which rotated off of the Earth-facing disk on 2025-12-31, and may be a region of interest seen in NSO/GONG data.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, using E115 as the source longitude as this CME is likely outflow from CME: 2026-01-11T22:45Z with E115 as its bulk. Longitude could easily vary +-10 degrees.

note: Bright, wide CME observed to the N in STEREO A COR2, NE in GOES CCOR-1, and NE in running difference imagery in real-time for SOHO LASCO C2/C3 (first observed as early as 2026-01-12T04:48Z, white light not available in real-time). The source is far-sided over the NE limb, first observed as wide, slow field line movement in SDO AIA 171/193 and filament ejecta in SDO AIA 304 starting around 2026-01-12T02:13Z. Post-eruptive arcades peeking over the NE limb also observed as early as 2026-01-12T07:30Z in GOES SUVI 195, with a brief SDO eclipse period from 2026-01-12T07:00Z-T08:00Z missing this observation.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-13T04:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME appears to be outflow linked to continued field line movement seen in SDO AIA 171 and 211 following M3.3 flare and resulting CME: 2026-01-11T22:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to source beyond the East limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate.

note: Hazy CME first seen to the South by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-13T06:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is field line movement seen to the South in SDO AIA 211 beginning at approximately 2026-01-13T04:19Z. Some movement in a Southeastern filament is also visible in SDO AIA 304 in association with this field line movement, but it does not appear that any material left the disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

note: Faint puff-like narrow outflow to the E with a small bright front in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This feature is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap.  There is no clear source for this CME/outflow feature.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement within SWPC_CAT using SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the north in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. It is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to an overnight data gap. The source appears to be a filament eruption best seen in GOES SUVI 304 beyond the northern limb starting around 2026-01-13T19:42Z. It is a far-sided eruption based on SDO/AIA and GOES SUVI imagery. The front of the CME appears faint in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 with a brief brighter core portion that lingers behind. This is likely due to the nature of the filament eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Best fit based on the possible SHOCK of the CME (fainter front ahead of bulk material) using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Longitude is estimated based on potential source location and best fit in available coronagraph imagery. Due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints, the parameters may be off by +/- 10 degrees.
Parameters based on best fit of potential BULK of CME. Tracking the brighter portion of the CME in available SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 difference imagery.

note: CME first seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-14T02:24Z. This front is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and to the NNW in STEREO A COR2. This CME is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-01-13T21:00Z which was associated with a far-sided filament eruption. No additional source signatures have been found.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

note: Very narrow, faint CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-14T05:00Z. The structure appears part was into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. CME is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is possibly related to a C9.6 class flare from an unnumbered region near/on the NE limb that peaked at 2026-01-14T03:50Z. The flare is best seen in SDO AIA 304 and SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Faint CME first seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-14T08:24Z. This front is also seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and is only speculatively by STEREO A COR2 due to poor imagery quality. A speculative source for this CME is a very minor eruption from AR 14340 (N14E16) best seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-01-14T06:18Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as a possible source location.

note: Faint CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-14T13:38Z. This CME is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and does not extend beyond the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. With low confidence, the source of this CME could be related to an eruption from an unnumbered region centered around N10E42 beginning around 2026-01-14T13:16Z. The eruption is associated with some brightening best seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 only. Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to source ambiguity.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-14T14:00Z, as well as by STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C3. The source of this CME is a small eruption denoted by a small region of dimming and field line movement seen in SDO AIA 193 centered at approx. N20E35 which began around 2026-01-14T13:09Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

note: Slow, wide, streamer blowout-like CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-01-14T17:12Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASOC C3 and to the SE in STEREO A COR2. It is also seen in GOES CCOR-1 but is partially obscured by the pylon. This CME experiences some northward deflection in later frames and speeds up after it reaches beyond the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. While measurement indicates that this CME is front-sided, there is no definitive source on the Earth-facing disk. A couple of source candidates include some minor field line movement in the far south best seen in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-01-14T13:00Z and an eruption from AR 14336 best seen in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-01-14T16:00Z

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). There are two possible sources on disk, however neither of these sources closely corresponds to the fit.

note: This CME is visible to the north in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery in real-time due to a data gap starting at 2026-01-14T16:23Z. The source may be related to an eruption and C3.8 flare from Active Region 14340 (N15E05) starting around 2026-01-14T23:40Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. Dimming is observed near the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 195 imagery. A subsequent EUV wave is observed traveling northwest of the eruption site. Noting that a coronal hole located southeast of Active Region 14340 may be responsible for northwestern deflection of this CME. The start of the CME is covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and after the data gap its front is very fuzzy/faint in COR2 coronagraph. The CME is also partially obscured by the pylon in STEREO LASCO C3.

cmeAnalyses.note: Final but somewhat approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by the location of the likely source (C-class flare in AR 14340 (N15E05)). The CME front is very fuzzy in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph, as most of its propagation is covered by a large daily data gap in STEREO A. The CME is also partially obscured by the pylon in STEREO LASCO C3.

Very preliminary measurement made with one coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) based on the source longitude. The measured parameters are speculative as this measurement relies on early imagery. Additionally, a triangulated measurement is not available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: Complex-shaped, bright halo CME with multiple (up to 3) fronts and a wide faint shock. Seen to NNW in SOHO LASCO/GOES CCOR-1 and to the NW in STEREO A COR2. Far-sided high N latitude source is clearly indicated by significant movement of field line in SDO AIA 171 to the NW starting after 2026-01-15T01:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of two main lobes of this complex-shaped CME in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).

note: CME with a wide faint shock and narrow bright bulk to the NE in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and to the N or NNE in STEREO A COR2. The initially bright bulk soon becomes faint after a couple of SOHO LASCO C2 timestamps. The source is likely the long two-pronged prominence seen to the NE in GOES SUVI 304 after approximately 2026-01-15T07:00Z. The prominence is likely obscured in SDO AIA 304 in two bad (all dark) images.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of this far-sided CME made with the fit in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO). The source (filament eruption) is behind the NE limb.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-15T23:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not currently visible in STEREO A COR2 due to an ongoing data gap beginning at 2026-01-15T16:23Z. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 centered at approximately N55W30 with liftoff beginning at approx. 2026-01-15T22:06Z. It appears a majority of this material fell back down, but some fully erupted.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

note: This CME is visible to the east in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not observed in real-time by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a planned data gap between downlink periods. The source is an eruption and C6.6 flare from Active Region 14345 (S17E58) starting around 2026-01-16T09:00Z seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. An opening of field lines is visible over the southeast limb around this time as seen in SDO AIA 193, GOES SUVI 195/284, and STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery. Due to a coronal hole located west of the source location, the resulting CME exhibits eastern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

note: Thin jet-like CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. This CME is much less than 10 degrees half width. The source appears to be AR 14336 (S09W87), as the observed filament ejecta in SDO AIA 171/304 starting around 2026-01-16T23:22Z starts near AR 14336 but sharply deflects NW then W along curved field lines.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane of sky due to heavy deflection from the source and no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate. CME half width is much less than 10 degrees, likely around 3-5 degrees maximum, limited analysis by the swpc_cat tool.

note: Jet-like CME visible to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source appears to be a C4.7 flare from AR 14336 just on/beyond the western limb in SDO/AIA 304 starting around 2026-01-17T02:22Z. The filament eruption is also visible in STEREO A EUVI 304 with possible deflection to the W due to a nearby coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 with a faint, fuzzy front overlapping with/following preceding faint outflow. The source could be one in a series of filament eruptions extending from AR 14341 (approximately S11E30) to AR 14342 (approximately N17E40) with filament liftoff and brightening seen in SDO AIA 304 starting at 2026-01-17T02:39Z. An alternate source is the opening/moving field lines seen on the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 between 2026-01-17T05:00Z and 2026-01-17T10:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis of an extremely fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2). There seems to be eastern deflection of over 10 degrees, as the likely source eruption has the longitude of ~40 degrees.

note: This CME is visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a filament eruption visible in all SDO/AIA wavelengths, in GOES SUVI wavelengths (including 304 and 284), and in STEREO A EUVI 304 and 195 starting around 2026-01-17T04:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is a small coronal hole to the east of the eruption location which may have caused deflection of the CME within the field of view.

note: Fuzzy front seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3/C2 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the SW in STEREO A COR2. In C2 there is a couple of consecutive fronts seen, with the later one catching up with the earlier one, so it is not easy to identify the start of the CME. No source has been found for this CME, so it is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative, approximate analysis based on the fit of this faint, fuzzy front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2). The source is likely far-sided and no coronal signatures have been found in EUV imagery.

note: CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. The source may be one of a series of filament eruptions extending from AR 14341 to AR 14342, centered around N10E40. One of these eruptions can be starting at 2026-01-17T07:34Z in GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis of an extremely fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2). There seems to be eastern deflection of over 10 degrees, as the likely source eruption has the longitude of ~40 degrees.

note: CME seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. The potential source may be one of a series of filament eruptions extending from AR 14341 to AR 14342, with a filament liftoff starting around 2026-01-17T10:22Z as seen in GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis of an extremely fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2). There seems to be eastern deflection of over 10 degrees, as the likely source eruption has the longitude of ~40 degrees.

note: Narrow jet-like CME seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a daily data gap. Its source could be the narrow minor prominence off or from behind the west limb seen at 2026-01-17T23:06Z in GOES SUVI 304 (and in SDO AIA 304).

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and its source is either on or behind the west limb, so no triangulation is possible.

note: Faint CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-18T01:36Z. This CME fades before exiting the C2 field-of-view, and is therefore not visible in SOHO LASCO C33, STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is an eruption from AR 14341 (S11E29) seen beginning at 2026-01-17T22:55Z, exhibiting very strong Northern deflection in SDO AIA 193 and 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-18T08:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. There is not clear source seen for this event as it is likely far sided, but is not clearly seen in STEREO A EUVI imagery either.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the Northeast in STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-01-18T09:38Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is an eruption from AR 14341 (S11E24) beginning at approx. 2026-01-18T07:55Z. This eruption is seen as dimming and field line opening seen in SDO AIA 193, 211, 304 and STEREO A EUVI 195 exhibiting a strong Northern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Halo CME first seen to the East by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-01-18T18:09Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 as a halo in later frames. This CME was initially covered by a routine scheduled downlink data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 which began at 2026-01-18T18:00Z but LASCO data became fully available later. The source of this CME is a long duration X1.9 flare from Active Region 14341 (S15E20), which peaked at 2026-01-18T18:09Z, with some possible contribution from Active Region 14345 (S16E27). This flare is visible in SDO AIA 94 and 131 and the associated eruption is seen as a wide region of dimming and field line opening in SDO AIA 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284, as well as very high and large area post eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 94, 304 and 193. Ejecta is seen in SDO AIA 193 and in STEREO A EUV 304 imagery (southwardly directed off the SE limb as seen from STEREO A). STEREO A 195 also sees it as a large opening of field lines close to the limb, around the equator.

note: Faint CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2, although it starts notably faint in LASCO C2's field of view and is much better viewed in later frames of the other coronagraphs. The source is not clear, and could be associated with a small filament ejecta centered around S05W85, NW of AR 14343, in SDO AIA 193/304 starting around 2026-01-22T00:31Z, although swpc_cat triangulation reliably matches longitudes closer to W110-W115 around the limb. STEREO A EUVI imagery is in a data gap between 2026-01-21T16:15Z and 2026-01-22T03:15Z, so no far-sided source observations were possible.

note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and more NE in STEREO A COR2. The source is likely a filament eruption in the vicinity of AR 14352, with complex dimming following the ejecta. The filament began to brighten and eject starting around 2026-01-22T01:06Z in SDO AIA 304, spanning approximately N07W12 to S02W03 at this time. Two dimming regions follow this ejecta in SDO AIA 193, one centered around N05E15 (referenced as the source coordinates here) directly after the filament ejecta, and another further away centered around N15E30 starting around 2026-01-22T02:04Z which may be part of this CME front or a following outflow. Wide field line movement deflecting to the NNE in SDO AIA 171 follows this complex eruption.

The source of this event is a filament eruption from along the NW limb (approx. N15W90). This eruption can be seen in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-01-22T17:22Z, and lifting off at approx. 2026-01-22T17:56Z. Field line opening is also visible in SDO AIA 171 and 193. The front in STEREO A is clear and bright in difference imagery, but there is also a fainter accompanying wider front as well in STEREO A, possibly a very faint shock. The source could be a minor filament eruption seen after 2026-01-23T02Z centered around (N30E01), as seen in GOES SUVI 304.

note: This CME is visible to the S/SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. It is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap. The source appears to be a complex eruption stretching from well beyond the SE limb to the Earth-facing side of the disk. Starting around 2026-01-23T20:36Z there is a filament eruption with associated moving/opening field lines, EUV wave, and post-eruptive arcades as seen in SDO/AIA 193, 171, 211, 304, and GOES SUVI 284 and 304 imagery. Large deflection to the south is visible in initial SOHO LASCO C2 frames. It is unclear what the cause of this deflection is, but it may be due to nearby coronal holes. This CME overlaps the CME first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-01-23T17:12Z which is associated with a similar portion of the filament eruption which may have helped trigger this complex eruption associated with this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The parameters may vary due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints.

note: Faint outflow/minor CME which is seen SE in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, as well as directly S in STEREO A COR2. The start of the CME could be a bit earlier and is unclear due to the faintness of the leading edge. No source has been found for this CME in EUV imagery, so it is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of this far-sided faint event based on the fit with three coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found in the EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

note: Narrow, faint CME seen to the NE in STEREO A COR2 and N in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Its source could be a relatively minor eruption just N of the northeastern coronal hole, centered ~(N15E10) which is seen starting around 2026-01-24T08:30Z in SDO AIA 193 as dimming and post-eruptive arcades.

cmeAnalyses.note: Analysis based on the fit of the narrow front with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3). Very well corresponds to the likely source - an eruption from (N15E10), with significant northern deflection, likely due to a large coronal hole being just to the south of the eruption site.

note: Faint, oblique front seen E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and possibly very faint dimpled and complex front to the E in STEREO A COR2. This front is better seen in SOHO LASCO and CCOR-1 but either very faint/complex or not seen in STEREO A COR2. Its source is possibly the clear movement of field lines on/behind the E limb seen in AIA 171/193 starting after 2026-01-24T08:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: A very speculative, approximate measurement due to the faintness of the front in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph. This analysis is based on the fit of the front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source (moving field lines indicating a far-sided filament eruption) is either on or (more likely) behind the E limb.

note: Faint CME with a dimpled, unclear front seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, overlapping with a fainter, wider and unclear front of CME:2026-01-24T09:24Z. Its source could be a very minor eruption/dimming seen around 2026-01-24T09:30Z around N05E80.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of a faint dimpled front based on its fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).

note: Relatively bright CME first seen to SSW in STEREO A COR2 and then S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Source eruption far to the south, behind the limb is seen in GOES SUVI 284 as movement of field lines after 2026-01-24T10:30Z and more faintly in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis of this far-sided CME, based on the fitting of the fronts seen in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). Fitting is difficult because the CME consists of several lobes.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the ESE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-24T12:48Z. This front is also seen to the ESE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and might be faintly seen to the SE in STEREO A COR2. There is no additional source signature for this CME, and it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-01-24T09:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of source signature and lack of available triangulation.

note: CME first seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-24T15:00Z. There is no additional source signature for this CME, and it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-01-24T10:53Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: Faint CME first seen to the S in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-24T18:36Z. There is no additional source signature for this CME, and it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-01-24T10:53Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen because of the lack of coronal signatures and because only one perspective was available in real time.

note: This CME is visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption visible over the eastern limb starting around 2026-01-25T05:15Z seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not observed by STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely an eruption and C6.3 flare from Active Region 14351 (S05W27) starting at 2026-01-25T10:25Z seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. Dimming is observed northwest of the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 imagery, indicating possibly northwestern deflection of this CME.

note: Faint CME seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is covered by a daily data gap in STEREO A COR2. A possible source is a faint, slow, medium-area eruption centered (N20E55) starting 2026-01-25T18:48Z, and seen in SDO AIA 193 as a faint, slowly developing dimming.

cmeAnalyses.note: A rather speculative, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis based on the possible source - a faint and slowly developing eruption seen in the NE of the solar disk in SDO AIA 193. The CME is fully covered by the daily gap in STEREO A COR2, so we are unable to confirm this analysis with triangulation.

note: CME with two fronts (possibly a faster shock and a slower bulk followed by multiple outflows) and seen to the SW in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is overed by a daily data gap in STEREO A COR2. Its source is likely a notable eruption in and around AR 14345 (S17W66) seen starting at 2026-01-25T19:20Z in SDO AIA 193 as dimming, minor moving ejecta and opening of field lines on/close to the SW limb. There is also a long duration C3.1 flare from this AR peaking at 2026-01-25T20:25Z and likely associated with the post-eruptive arcades of this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis, based on the location of the source - flare and eruption in/around AR 14345. The CME is fully covered by the daily gap in STEREO A COR2, so we are unable to confirm this analysis with triangulation.

note: Faint CME seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but covered by a daily data gap in STEREO A COR2. A possible source for this CME is C7.9 flare north of AR 14342 centered ~(N18W70) peaking at 2026-01-25T21:36Z, accompanied by a very minor eruption signified by small post-eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Somewhat speculative one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO) analysis, based on the location of the source - flare and eruption from AR 14342. The CME is fully covered by the daily gap in STEREO A COR2, so we are unable to confirm this analysis with triangulation.

note: This CME is faintly visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption from Active Region 14350 (N18W17) starting around 2026-01-26T04:00Z seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195 imagery. Dimming is observed near the eruption site around this time in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 195 imagery. Additionally, post eruption arcades begin to form around 2026-01-26T04:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters account for northern deflection from a nearby coronal hole.

note: This CME is visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in real-time by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a planned data gap between downlink periods starting at 2026-01-26T16:24Z or in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-01-26T17:08Z. The source is likely a far-sided eruption and opening of field lines visible from beyond the eastern limb starting around 2026-01-26T16:40Z in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location. Additionally, due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery a triangulated measurement was not available for this event.

note: This CME is faintly seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. There is a data gap in STEREO A COR2 during this event. The source of this CME appears to be a filament eruption centered near N45E60 beginning to lift off around 2026-01-26T16:00Z as seen in SDO/AIA 304. This eruption appears to cause a sympathetic eruption beyond the limb of another filament eruption (best seen in SDO/AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 304) which may have contributed to the shape of this event in early frames of SOHO LASCO C2.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using the approximate source location and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

note: Streamer blowout CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-27T01:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is seen in GOES SUVI 284 as wide rising and opening field lines beginning around 2026-01-26T22:42Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-27T01:48Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-01-26T17:08Z to 2026-01-27T03:23Z. The source of this event is a small eruption seen on the Western limb beginning at approximately 2026-01-27T00:29Z. A small amount of material can be seen leaving the disk in SDO AIA 304, along with field line opening visible in SDO AIA 193. This source is not visible in STEREO A EUVI imagery due to the same data gap as COR2A.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to source location near the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate. Range of longitude fits between W70 and W120.

note: CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-27T10:36Z, as well as by STEREO A COR2 in later frames. The source of this event is a C2.9 class flare from beyond the SE limb which peaked at 2026-01-27T09:43Z. This flare can be seen in SDO AIA 131, along with field line opening and post eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. This fit suggests a high Southern deflection.

note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is also visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 after a real-time data gap from 2026-01-27T15:38Z to 2026-01-28T03:23Z. The potential source may be an eruption with opening field lines beyond the west limb best in SDO AIA 193/GOES SUVI 284 starting at 2026-01-28T23:31Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on potential source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The eruption is also visible as opening field lines in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Limited imagery was available for this measurement due to the faintness of the leading edge of the CME.

note: CME visible to the NE in GOES CCOR-1, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and STEREO A COR2. The source is likely a filament eruption on the east limb as best seen in GOES SUVI 304 imagery, with liftoff starting at 2026-01-28T12:28Z. Opening field lines are also seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary measurement based on the potential source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

note: Very faint CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-01-28T21:12Z. This front is also faintly seen in GOES CCOR-1, but it is too "fuzzy" to be measurable. There is not good source candidate for this CME on the Earth-facing disk. The source may be far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2.

note: Extremely faint front first seen to the NE part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-01-28T21:48Z. This CME is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 for three frames of running difference imagery. It is also seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1. The CME is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. It is too faint to discern in white light. There is a small filament eruption from AR 14359 (N20E30) best seen in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-01-28T20:30Z that could be related.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on speculative source around AR 14359 (N20E30).

note: Narrow CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 around 2026-01-28T22:36Z. This CME is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the NNW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-29T02:00Z. This CME fades before exiting the field-of-view in SOHO LASCO C2, and is therefore not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1. This CME is also not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-01-28T15:53Z to 2026-01-29T03:23Z. The source of this event is faint field line opening seen along the NW limb in SDO AIA 193 beginning around 2026-01-29T01:00Z, exhibiting some possible Northern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to faint source near the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate or STEREO A EUVI to observe exact source longitude. Longitudes are fitting between W50 and W130.

note: CME first seen to the SSE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-29T04:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. No source is visible, as source is likely far sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-01-29T08:24Z, as well as by STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a minor eruption seen along the SE limb near Active Region 14362 (approx. S20E80) seen beginning around 2026-01-29T07:52Z in SDO AIA 304, with field line opening also seen in SDO AIA 171.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the SSE by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2026-01-29T12:45Z, as well as to the SSW in STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real time due to a downlink data gap which began at 2026-01-29T08:24Z. The source of this event is field line opening seen beyond the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-01-29T11:10Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the NW by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2026-01-29T15:00Z, as well as by STEREO A COR2 in later frames.
