This flare is visible in SDO AIA 131, along with field line opening seen in SDO AIA 171, 193 and 211.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

note: Faint, narrow CME seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 and very faintly in a few early images of C3. Fully covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A COR2. Its source could be a minor prominence off/from just behind the E limb best seen in GOES SUVI 304/284 and also seen in SDO AIA 304/131/193/171 starting 2026-01-29T21:03Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME source is either the E limb or behind the E limb. This CME is fully covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A imagery. Therefore, the only analysis possible is plane-of-sky, using SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 imagery

note: Faint CME with a messy partial halo front seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A. Its source is likely the movement/opening of field lines behind the SE limb best seen after 2026-01-29T22:40Z in GOES SUVI 284 and also in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated, triangulated measurement based on later frames including STEREO A COR2.

The CME is far-sided, with the behind-the-limb source indicated by movement/opening of field lines seen in EUV imagery. The CME is covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A imagery, so no triangulation is possible. Plane-of-sky analysis is the only analysis available for this event

note: Narrow, jet-like CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 staring around 2026-01-30T13:26Z. This CME is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this narrow, jet-like CME is most likely associated with some field line rising near the NE limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 beginning around 2026-01-30T11:58Z. Some small post-eruptive arcades are also visible around an unnumbered region located around N15E70 in SDO AIA 193 beginning around 2026-01-30T13:00Z. There is some northward deflection evident particularly once the CME reaches the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulated measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source location near the NE limb.

note: CME observed to the E only in 3 available frames of STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-01-30T18:38Z. Not visible in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C2/C3, perhaps too faint or obscured by the occulting disk. No distinct source for this CME is observed on-disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 plane-of-sky at E38.2, with STEREO A's position at W51.8.

note: Diffuse, faint CME observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source may be filament ejecta just on/beyond the east limb starting around 2026-01-30T23:21Z in SDO AIA 304, deflecting northward.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 plane-of-sky, with no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate and a source likely just beyond the east limb with northward deflection.

note: Fast, asymmetric CME with a front best seen in SOHO LASCO imagery but also clear in running difference imagery in all coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). Associated with the occulted C6.0 flare from former Active Region 14351 (S04W107) from behind the W limb in SDO, peaking at 2026-01-31T04:54Z, and an eruption with opening of field lines seen in SDO AIA 193/171 starting 2026-01-31T04:50Z. The flare and eruption are best seen (as dimming, post-eruptive arcades and an EUV wave) in STEREO A EUV 195/304 in the Active Region 14351 (located behind the W limb in SDO, at S04W55 as seen from STEREO A), making the source location coordinates S04W107.

cmeAnalyses.note: Analysis based on the location of the source (C6.0 flare and eruption in the former Active Region 14351, seen in STEREO A EUV imagery after 2026-01-31T04:50Z.

note: A very faint, minor CME seen to the W in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). Source for this event has not been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate, speculative analysis based on the fit with 3 coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found in EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

note: Faint CME with a pointy, dimpled front to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, partially obscured by a pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The corresponding super faint, messy front is possibly seen to the NE in STEREO A COR2 in a few limited timeframes. The source of this CME could be a very small eruption just E of former AR 14365 (~N28W20), seen in SDO starting at 2026-01-31T13:49Z as minor dimming and small size post-eruptive arcades.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the possible source, with some minor deflection westwards from the source eruption.

note: CME first observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2 before a data gap begins at 2026-01-31T19:38Z preventing a good view of front development, then later to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, starting at 2026-01-31T22:12Z in LASCO C2. With an ongoing SOHO LASCO campaign reducing data quality and the STEREO A COR2 gap, no overlapping observations of the front are possible in real-time, but there is good confidence in this being the same front due to an observed far-sided source and no other on-disk sources at this time. The far-sided source is observed in STEREO A EUVI 304/195 starting around 2026-01-31T16:15Z on the west limb as viewed from STEREO A, putting it around W141.8 with the spacecraft currently at W51.8.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D single spacecraft analysis with SOHO LASCO C3 and one LASCO C2 frame, using the observed source around W141.8 in STEREO A EUVI imagery as the basis, and the longitude could easily  vary +-10 degrees. Analysis is quite speculative due to LASCO data quality with an ongoing campaign, STEREO A COR2 data gap, and LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 fronts not aligning well with the lemniscate tool.
SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only plane-of-sky. Considering this analysis true with the speculative nature of the other 3D single spacecraft analysis, due to the factors noted within that analysis entry.

note: Faint CME with a diffuse leading edge observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and briefly in STEREO A COR2 before a data gap. The source is very likely a C7.0 class flare (N16E56) from AR 14366 peaking at 2026-01-31T18:33Z observed in SDO AIA 131, eruption also observed as field line movement, dimming region centered around N23E58, and surface brightening in SDO AIA 171/193/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft 3D analysis with limited SOHO LASCO C3 frames due to an ongoing campaign, also due to poor LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 image quality and availability. Uses the flare source at N16E56 and dimming source at N23E58 finding some northward deflection, although some variance in the latitude and longitude is expected with the single-spacecraft analysis.

note: Narrow CME visible to the NE in STEREO A COR2 imagery. It is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 nor GOES CCOR-1 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME, but it is possibly associated with flaring activity from Active Region 14366 located in the NE of the Earth-facing disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using -38 degrees for longitude based on STEREO A's location at time of CMEs first appearance in STEREO A imagery.

note: This CME is faintly seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for the CME however it is possible it is associated with flaring activity from Active Region 14366 in the NE of the Earth-facing disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This measurement includes a very faint wider front visible in SOHO LASCO C2 frames. The brightest portion of this CME is visible along the southern edge of the CME with a much more narrow appearance. This CME in STEREO A is more clearly defined in difference imagery.

note: CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-01T12:48Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is fast rising loops and opening field lines seen along the SE limb in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284 beginning at approx. 2026-02-01T10:53Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Complex CME consisting of multiple overlapping fronts, the first of which is first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-02T00:48Z, as well as in GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The start of this event is covered in STEREO A COR2 by a data gap which began at 2026-02-01T14:53Z and ended at 2026-02-02T03:23Z, but after the data gap at least one lobe of this CME is clearly seen to the ENE in STEREO A COR2 in multiple timestamps. The source of this event is an X8.1 flare from Active Region 14366 (N13E35) that peaked at 2026-02-01T23:57Z and possibly also the following X2.8 flare from the same AR peaking at 2026-02-02T00:36Z. The flares are visible in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 131. Two eruptions of filament ejecta are also seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 in association with these flares, the earlier ejecta more northwardly directed and seemingly deflecting towards the west, and the ejecta after the second flare which is not as northward as the initial one.

cmeAnalyses.note: Final, approximate and speculative analysis based on the fitting with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) once STEREO A COR2 imagery became available after the end of the daily data gap in STEREO A. This analysis leaves out the earliest, more northward, faint front seen in SOHO C2 and CCOR-1 as the likely corresponding very faint front in the first couple of available STEREO A COR2 after the data gap is very unclear/obscured by brighter following front. The CME is treated as one event in this analysis. The fitting indicates a likely significant deflection westwards from the source location (location of the X-class flares from Active Region 14366), possibly due to the effect of the coronal hole to the NE of the Active Region location. Analysis is complicated by the asymmetrical shape of the CME in STEREO A COR2, the fact that the start of the CME is obscured by the data gap in STEREO and the difficulty separating multiple overlapping fronts in SOHO and CCOR-1.

Preliminary measurement with one-coronagraph analysis method, using early GOES CCOR-1 frames before SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 imagery became available for this event, based on the approximate source location (X8 flare).

note: CME/outflow seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and (most clearly) in STEREO A COR2 following and overlapping with CME: 2026-02-02T00:48Z. (Its start in LASCO might be somewhat different). This is the most distinct outflow front among the multiple follow ups to CME: 2026-02-02T00:48Z. It is especially distinct in STEREO A COR2, as it seems to have a higher latitude than the CME: 2026-02-02T00:48Z. Its source is not clear but could be the long-duration M5.2 flare from AR 4366 (N13E35) peaking at 2026-02-02T02:51Z as seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative analysis based on the fit of the front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). It is not clear which of the multiple fronts in SOHO/CCOR-1 corresponds to the more clear front in STEREO A. The possible source (flare from AR 14366) is more eastward than the longitude we are obtaining from triangulation, so there might be westward deflection of the CME.

note: Faint narrow CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. It is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source appears to be an M-class flare from Active Region 14366 in the NE peaking at 2026-02-02T11:24Z as seen in SDO/AIA 131. There is a coronal hole near this Active Region which may have contributed to deflection of the CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There may be some uncertainty with this fit due to the faintness of the CME.

note: This CME is visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. It is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-02-02T15:23Z. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on plane-of-sky fit of CME feature using the SWPC_CAT measuring tool. There is a fainter wider portion not included in this measurement due to difficulty in seeing it. It is more notable in white-light imagery.

note: Extremely faint CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-02T20:36Z. This CME may also been seen in a couple of "fuzzy" frames from GOES CCOR-1. The front dissipates before reaching the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is possibly an eruption from AR 14366 best seen in SDO AIA 193 beginning around 2026-02-02T20:25Z followed by an EUV wave. The eruption was followed by an M3.3 class flare from AR 14366 that peaked at 2026-02-02T21:08Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: There is a high degree of uncertainty in this measurement due to a speculative source and a lack of available triangulation.

note: CME first seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-03T03:36Z. The CME is also seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the NNW in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely far-sided. There is some ejecta seen over the NNE limb best seen in GOES SUVI 304 beginning around 2026-02-03T02:00Z and some moving field lines from beyond the NNE limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-02-03T01:50Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

note: This CME is visible to the west/northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source may be related to a slow-to-lift off filament eruption starting around 2026-02-03T15:00Z which spans ~N20W30 to ~N00W45 as seen in SDO AIA 193/304 imagery. A subsequent dimming is observed in SDO AIA 193 imagery around this time which appears to move westward.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption and brightening centered near S18E60 starting around 2026-02-04T11:52Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption centered near S05E60 starting around 2026-02-04T12:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/193/284/304 imagery. Dimming is observed in SDO AIA 193 imagery at this time, and an opening of field lines can be observed over the eastern limb in GOES SUVI 284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption centered near N20E23 starting around 2026-02-04T14:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery. A subsequent dimming is seen in SDO AIA 193 which appears to deflect northeast of the eruption site.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-04T18:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-04T15:53Z. The source of this CME was not found in real-time in EUV imagery. Some of the CME material reverts back towards the solar disk and the rest of the material continues to propagate out into the corona. This separation can be seen beginning around 2026-02-04T18:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the Southwest by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-04T20:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-04T15:53Z. The source of this CME is field line opening seen along the SW limb (approx. S21W85) in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-02-04T19:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-04T20:48Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-04T15:53Z. The source of this is filament ejecta seen originating from beyond the Eastern limb in SDO AIA 304 beginning at approx. 2026-02-04T18:00Z, along with field line opening seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to source originating beyond the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate. Longitudes are fitting between E50 and E115.

note: This CME is visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap from 2026-02-04T15:53Z to 2026-02-05T03:38Z. The source is likely related to an eruption on the eastern limb (N05E90) starting around 2026-02-04T23:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 171/195/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

note: Faint CME observed to the NW only in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, not observed in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 perhaps due to faintness of the front and/or a GOES CCOR-1 eclipse period. The source is a small filament located near AR 14359 (around N22W65), beginning to lift off around 2026-02-05T03:36Z observed as filament ejecta and brightening in SDO AIA 304 and field line movement in SDO AIA 171. The concurrent M2.5 and M2.7 flares from AR 14366 at this time are unrelated, and there is no exceptional brightening observed in SDO AIA 131 coupled to this eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only 3D measurement using the eruptive source at N22W65, finding a good fit with minimal deflection, although some variation in the latitude/longitude is possible without triangulation here. LASCO C3's pylon obscures the faint front in running difference imagery.

note: Faint CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not observed in available STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source for this CME is observed as a helmet streamer breaking open over the west limb in GOES SUVI 171 starting around 2026-02-05T03:54Z, however it is difficult to trace down the latiude and longitude corresponding to the center of this feature.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 plane-of-sky due to uncertain central coordinates of the observed streamer feature in GOES SUVI imagery. However, it is likely within W70-W110. Half width slightly less than 10 degrees.

note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and to the southeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely related to a C6.0 flare and eruption from Active Region 14362 (S18W15) starting around 2026-02-05T12:50Z which exhibits southeast deflection as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: This CME is faint but visible to the south in SOHO LASCO C2 and to the southeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery for a few frames before becoming too diffuse to observe. The source may be related to a dimming region entered near S33E10 starting around 2026-02-05T13:00Z in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: CME observed to the S/SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, greatly obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1, and to the SE of STEREO A COR2 for 3 frames before a data gap begins at 2026-02-05T16:08Z. The source is likely associated with an M1.8 flare from AR 14362 (S18W17) which peaked at 2026-02-05T15:13Z, observed in SDO AIA 131. Following this flare, a large but faint southerly deflected EUV wave is observed causing a wide swath of dimming and field line wiggling to the south of this region observed in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 284, centered around S45W10 and spanning approximately E25-W30 and S30-S65. Multiple concentric fronts emerge in LASCO C2 following the intial 2026-02-05T15:48Z front, but these are considered part of the same complex bulk.

cmeAnalyses.note: BULK triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and three available STEREO A COR2 frames before its data gap. Multiple concentric fronts are observed in LASCO C2, treated as one front measuring on the leading most edge and capturing the full width of the brighter material.

SHOCK front analysis, of the very faint shock feature observed in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and even more faintly in STEREO A COR2. This feature is trackable enough in running difference imagery to get a triangulation, but difficult to properly discern the width, so this analysis is presented with some speculation but a good 3D fit between coronagraphs.

note: CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-05T20:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and to the SE in STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is not visible in GOES CCOR-1 due to being blocked by the pylon. The source of this event is an M2.2 flare from Active Region 14362 (S18W19) which peaked at 2026-02-05T22:17Z. A faint EUV wave and field line movement can be seen in SDO AIA 171/193 following this flare propagating towards the Southwest, suggesting a possible SW deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This fit suggests a SW deflection. Feature appears wider in SOHO LASCO than it does in STEREO A COR2.

note: Halo CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-05T23:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not currently visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-05T23:38Z. The source of this event is far sided, as wide opening field lines can be seen beyond the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284. A far sided active region can be seen in NSO GONG located at approx. E150 degrees longitude, that may be responsible for this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement using a few frames of STEREO Ahead.
Preliminary measurement using early frames based on best fit in SOHO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Faint CME visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME. The CME is not visible in GOES CCOR-1 due to the placement of the control arm.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: Faint CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-06T19:38Z. This eruption is also visible in GOES SUVI 284 and 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary plane-of-sky measurement using first available frames in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

note: Very faint narrow CME with two or three consecutive fronts (maybe the earliest one is a narrow shock), which is seen directly to the W in SOHO LASCO C2, and very faintly seen in C3. It is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. No clear source for this event has been found in EUV imagery, so it is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A imagery and its source has not been found in EUV imagery, so only a plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

note: CME/streamer blowout with a very faint, fuzzy front seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 and in SOHO LASCO C3. Its start is likely earlier, as in early COR2 and C3 timestamps this faint front is very hard to trace and it is not clearly seen in C2. The CME is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. No source has been found for this CME in EUV imagery, so it is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate, speculative analysis based on an approximate fit of the very faint, fuzzy, uncertain front in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3.

note: A relatively bright narrow CME seen to the NNW in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1), partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. Its source may be a minor eruption right behind the NW limb as seen (rather unclearly) in SDO AIA/GOES SUVI 304. Minor opening of field lines are also seen behind the limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-02-07T09:00Z. This eruption is most clearly seen in STEREO A EUV 195 as slowly developing dimming centered around (N35W90) starting after 2026-02-07T09:00ZZ.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the minor source eruption seen mostly in STEREO A EUVI 195 as minor dimming. There seems to be a northern deflection from the source location.

note: Faint CME to the NNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 (partially obscured by pylon in C3), directed to the N in STEREO A COR2A, following and overlapping with 2026-02-07T09:36Z CME. No clear source found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Analysis based solely on the fit of the faint, fuzzy front in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source has been found in EUV imagery.

note: CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 in real-time due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-07T16:38Z. The potential source may be seen as moving field lines in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA starting around 2026-02-07T19:21Z. A possible far-sided source candidate may be a region of interest seen in NSO GONG data, which may be former Active Region 14341, located approximately around longitude -145.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location in NSO GONG data.

note: Faint narrow CME/jet seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is fully covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A COR2. No clear source has been found in EUV imagery for this event.

The potential source may be an eruption with dimming centered around N15W50 best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting at 2026-02-08T23:04Z.

note: CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-09T23:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-02-09T18:53Z to 2026-02-10T03:38Z. The source of this CME is a C9.2 flare from Active Region 14374 (N10E75) which peaked at 2026-02-09T23:02Z, along with a small amount of associated filament ejecta exhibiting a heavy Southern deflection. This flare is visible in SDO AIA 131, along with field line movement seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and ejecta visible in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

The source of this event is a small eruption from Active Region 14374 (N11E67) which began around 2026-02-10T09:14Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location. This feature appears to exhibit a strong Northern deflection.

It is not clearly seen in GOES CCOR-1. There is a data gap in STEREO A COR during this event. The source for this CME appears to be a small and narrow filament eruption with associated moving/opening field lines visible in SDO/AIA 304, 171, and 193; as well as in GOES SUVI 284, to the SW along the limb starting around 2026-02-10T19:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on potential source location area and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. This is specifically measuring the most narrow portion of the feature visible in difference imagery. There is a much slower, slightly wider portion that may also be associated with this source that measured with speeds below 150 km/s. The longitude of this CME may vary by +/- 10 degrees due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints of the source location.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

note: This CME is visible to the northwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is visible over the northwest limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location. As this event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs, a triangulated measurement was not available for this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

note: This CME is visible to the north in STEREO A COR2 imagery and to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source for this event is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: CME first seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-13T17:00Z. This CME is also seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C3 but is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely related to some field line movement seen beyond the SSW limb in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-02-13T16:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 using SWPC_CAT.

note: This feature is first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-13T19:36Z. It dissipates before reaching the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 or GOES CCOR-1 and is covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. The CME is only seen for four frames in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmeAnalyses.note: This CME only appears in four frames from SOHO LASCO C2, and the measurement has a higher degree of uncertainty.

note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption observed on/near the northeast limb in SDO AIA 304 imagery starting around 2026-02-14T06:20Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the approximate source location.

note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely an eruption near the northeast limb starting around 2026-02-14T12:40Z seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

note: CME observed faintly to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. No distinct source for this CME is observed in available SDO AIA or GOES SUVI imagery, so it is likely that the source could be far-sided around the west limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only plane-of-sky due to lack of observed source location and STEREO A COR2 imagery for triangulation.

note: CME observed faintly to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. No distinct source for this CME is observed in available SDO AIA or GOES SUVI imagery, so it is likely that the source could be far-sided around the west limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only plane-of-sky due to lack of observed source location and STEREO A COR2 imagery for triangulation. Half width in early frames less than 10 degrees, expands to just fit 10 degrees as it propogates.

note: CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source location is not directly observed, but seen in GOES SUVI 171/195/284 as field line movement over the E limb starting around 2026-02-14T22:02Z, indicating a likely far-sided source over the east limb. Far-sided NSO GONG data does not currently show any far-sided regions in this vicinity.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk plane-of-sky with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, due to the unknown far-sided source location and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate.

Shock plane-of-sky with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, due to the unknown far-sided source location and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate. Analyzing the slightly wider, faint front preceeding the bright inner bulk following it.

note: Narrow, jet-like CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-15T07:48Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not visible in STEREO A COR2. The source is possibly related to some minor field line movement from beyond the NE limb best seen in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-02-15T05:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen as the CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 and the source candidate is not on the Earth-facing disk.

note: CME first seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-15T10:36Z. It is also seen to the SSW in STEREO A COR2 but is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. This CME is overtaken by following, faster CME: 2026-02-15T12:24Z before it reaches the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3. A possible source candidate for this CME is some opening field lines seen near the SSW limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-02-15T09:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME followed by multiple follow-up fronts that is first seen to the WSW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-15T12:00Z. There are a few source candidates for this CME. The first is opening field lines beyond the SW limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 beginning around 2026-02-15T10:50Z. This eruption is also seen in STA EUVI 195 starting around 2026-02-15T11:25Z from the vicinity of former AR 14370 (S18W95). Another option is an eruption from an unnumbered region SE of AR 14373 beginning as early as 2026-02-15T07:30Z in SDO AIA 193. It is in very close proximity to a coronal hole, so westward deflection in likely.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and it has multiple potential sources, so only plane-of-sky analysis is definitive.

note: CME first seen to the SW partially into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-15T12:24Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in GOES CCOR-1, SOHO LASCO C3, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely a filament eruption from on/over the SW limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-02-15T10:50Z. This filament eruption can also be seen in STA EUVI 195 starting around 2026-02-15T11:25Z from the vicinity of former AR 14371. The filament and associated, subsequent dimming spans approximately W85 to W100.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There is some degree of uncertainty in this measurement, and longitudes ranging from W77 to W90 were measured.

note: Narrow, jet-like CME briefly seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1. The CME does not appear is SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen because this feature cannot be triangulated and does not have visible a source candidate.

note: CME first faintly seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-16T03:36Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. It is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The leading edge appears much wider in STEREO A COR2. The source of the CME is likely far-sided beyond the SW limb. There is some field line movement near the SW limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting as early as 2026-02-15T23:00Z. The start time of the CME is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A EUVI, but some fluctuation of field lines and some brightening can be seen to the far west in STEREO A EUVI 195 when data resumes at 2026-02-16T03:55Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is very faint and difficult to track in SOHO LASCO C2, so only SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 were used for the measurement.

note: Large CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-16T04:24Z. This CME is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and as a partial halo in STEREO A COR2 with the bulk to the E. The CME is also seen to the E in GOES CCOR-1, but the majority of its propagation is obscured by Earth-shine. There appears to be some northward deflection very early in the propagation of the CME in the SOHO LASCO C2 FOV. The source of this CME is associated with an occulted M2.4 flare from beyond the E limb (~S10) that peaked at 2026-02-16T04:35Z. The associated eruption and ejecta can be seen across SDO AIA wavelengths, but the flare is best seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit of the SHOCK feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-16T05:00Z. This CME is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the SW in STEREO A COR2. This CME is likely outflow following CME: 2026-02-16T04:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO C2/C3 measurement based on longitude of previous CME: 2026-02-16T04:24Z.

note: Slow loop-shaped streamer blowout CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-16T11:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. It is followed by multiple loop-like follow-up fronts.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found for this CME, confirming its far-sidedness.

note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-16T11:36Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 but is mostly obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is most likely an eruption seen near the WSW limb in STA EUVI 195 starting as early as 2026-02-16T09:00Z. The eruption is best seen as some material leaving the disk followed post eruptive arcades.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the E in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-02-16T13:23Z. This CME is also seen as a faint, partial halo in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is most likely a large eruption best seen in SDO AIA 171 and SDO AIA 304 starting around 2026-02-16T12:18Z spanning S30E15 to S10W05. Subsequent dimming and an EUV wave can also be seen in SDO AIA 193, along with a C1.0 flare seen peaking at 2026-02-16T13:07Z in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit of the leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The CME is best visible in STEREO A COR2.

note: CME first seen to the E in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-02-16T14:08Z. This CME is also visible as a very faint partial halo in SOHO LASCO C2 following earlier, faster CME: 2026-02-16T13:23Z. The source of this CME is possibly related to the same eruption that resulted in CME: 2026-02-16T13:23Z. The main eruption is best seen in SDO AIA 171 and SDO AIA 304 starting around 2026-02-16T12:18Z spanning S30E15 to S10W05. There are a couple of smaller portions of the eruption that could be responsible for this CME. The first is an area of dimming that is deflected slightly to the north best seen in SDO AIA 193. The second is an area of dimming south of AR 14373 that follows the main eruption. It is also best seen in SDO AIA 193. In real time, the original analysis of this CME was complicated by gaps in the SOHO and STEREO A downlink schedules.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary analysis based on best fit between limited frames from SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

note: Faint, narrow, jet-like CME seen to the ESE in SOHO LASCO C2 and in early C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and not visible in STEREO A COR2. No source has been found in EUV imagery, so it is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A coronagraph imagery and no source for it has been found in EUV imagery, so only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

note: Faint jet-like CME seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs and likely mostly obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Its source is not clear: the only potential source seems to be too early - a slow eruption close to the SW limb in STA EUV 304/195 (also seen as ejecta/opening of field lines in SDO AIA 171 (~2026-02-17T07:30Z). STA EUV 304 imagery might indicate that part of the eruption departed the disk later, around 2026-02-17T09:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the approximate location of the source eruption, as seen in STEREO A EUV 195/304 imagery and on the fit of this narrow CME in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO coronagraphs.

note: Streamer blowout CME first seen to the Southeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-17T11:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is field line movement seen along the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-02-17T09:55Z, followed by hours of slowly appearing/spreading faint but wide post-eruptive arcades in the SW quarter of SDO AIA imagery, expanding westward from the SE limb and eventually obscuring the NE boundary of the SE part of the large elongated coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-17T18:00Z. It is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this event is field line opening and breaking seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA 171/193 beginning at 2026-02-17T17:04Z with full breaking seen at 2026-02-17T18:16Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement restricted to plane-of-sky because this CME is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2 and STEREO A EUVI.

note: Faint CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T00:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-17T16:06Z. The source of this event is slight dimming and restructuring of field lines seen located at approx. S50E50 in SDO AIA 171/193 beginning at approx. 2026-02-17T23:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the SSW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-18T01:25Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LSCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. It is overtaken by the much larger, faster CME: 2026-02-18T04:48Z early in the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3. It is also obscured by Earth-shine in GOES CCOR-1.  The source of this CME is most likely a filament eruption near the SW limb. The filament is best seen in SDO AIA 304 and can be seen starting to rise as early as 2026-02-17T16:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

note: Narrow, jet-like CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-18T03:48Z. This CME is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. No independent source candidate has been found for this CME, and it is possibly outflow following streamer blowout CME: 2026-02-17T11:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found for this CME.

note: CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-02-18T04:48Z. This CME is also seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely related to a large filament eruption near former AR 14371. Filamentary material can be seen erupting beyond the SW limb in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-02-18T04:10Z. A large opening of field lines can also be seen beyond the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 and SDO AIA 193. The same eruption can be more clearly seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting around 2026-02-18T03:55Z and later around 2026-02-18T04:45Z in STEREO A EUVI 304 after resumption of data after a real-time data gap.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit of the leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. There is some uncertainty in these parameters as longitudes ranging from ~W115 to ~W131 yield reasonable measurements.

note: Faint CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-18T09:24Z. This CME is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. This feature is most likely outflow following streamer blowout CME: 2026-02-17T11:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

note: Faint, irregularly shaped front seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1, overlapping with the continuous outflows following CME: 2026-02-18T04:48Z. The source could be a very slowly developing eruption of a filament centered around (S25W100) that is seen in STEREO A EUV 304 going for hours after 2026-02-18T07Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of this front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).

note: A slow and faint minor CME overlapping with the outflow after CME: 2026-02-18T04:48Z, seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, and possibly super faintly in C3 and GOES CCOR-1. There is no clear source eruption seen in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the fit of this narrow front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2). No clear source has been found in the EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

note: Bright CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T19:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-18T15:53Z. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-02-18T18:12Z. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 195 and 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using longitude of E90 due to source beyond the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.

note: CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T20:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-18T15:53Z. The source of this event is a long duration C2.2 flare located as approx. (S07E60) which peaked at 2026-02-18T20:42 as seen in GOES SUVI 131. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 171 and 193 exhibiting a possible Northern deflection. This CME partially overlaps with CME: 2026-02-18T21:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-18T21:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-02-18T15:53Z. The source of this event is field line opening seen from beyond the East limb in GOES SUVI 284 beginning at 2026-02-18T20:27Z. This event partially overlaps with CME: 2026-02-18T20:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to source from beyond the East limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.

note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap. The source is likely related to an eruption from Active Region 14374 (N08W58) starting around 2026-02-19T10:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery during this event, a stereoscopic measurement was not available. The measured parameters may therefore vary as a result.

note: Narrow, faint, fast, jet-like CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2. Covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A. Its source could be the minor ejecta close to the SE limb seen after 2026-02-19T18:50Z in SDO AIA 304, accompanied by the movement/opening of field lines on the SE limb seen in AIA SDO 193/171. Following this, there is row of post-eruptive arcades seen very close to the SE limb (S27E80) in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by a night time data gap in STEREO A. The source of this CME is not clear. So only a plane-of-sky analysis with SOHO LASCO imagery is possible.

note: A narrow, faint, fast, jet-like CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2. Covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A. The source is not clear, as there is a lot of activity close/on/behind the SE limb: it could be a minor ejecta close to the SE limb seen after 2026-02-19T18:50Z in SDO AIA 304, although it is too early. There is also a movement/opening of field lines on the SE limb seen in AIA SDO 193/171 after 2026-02-19T20:30Z, following which there is a row of post-eruptive arcades seen very close to the SE limb (S27E80) in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by a night time data gap in STEREO A. The source of this CME is not clear. So only a plane-of-sky analysis with SOHO LASCO imagery is possible.

note: Narrow CME seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not visible in STEREO A COR2. The source may be an ejecta of material beyond the NNW limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery at 2026-02-20T04:44Z. The eruption is also visible on the limb in SDO AIA 171 imagery in ISWA. The source is not visible in STEREO A EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

note: This CME is visible to the N/NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source of this CME is visible in SDO/AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 304 to the N/NE beyond the limb starting around 2026-02-21T21:10Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. An extremely faint shock may be seen as a partial southern halo in STEREO A imagery as well. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

note: Lightbulb shaped CME seen to the SE in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). It is, save a couple of early images, largely covered by the nighttime data gap in the STEREO A COR2 coronagraph. Its source is a large filament eruption centered near S25E60 which begins to erupt around 2026-02-22T15:00Z, as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304, with dimming and post-eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis based on the later timestamps of GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 coronagraphs and fitting the early timestamps of STEREO A COR2. The CME is seen speeding up in later timestamps.

note: Faint CME with a fuzzy front seen to the W in GOES CCOR-1. The CME is fully covered by the night time data gap in STEREO A COR2. Its source is a C1.5 flare and associated eruption near the southwest limb (~S20W80) which begins to fully lift off around 2026-02-22T16:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is observed over the southwest limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/193/284 imagery at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis with one-coronagraph (GOES CCOR-1) method, based on the location of the source eruption. The CME is not seen in SOHO LASCO coronagraph because of the scheduled data gap that has not yet backfilled and in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph due to the night time data gap.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-02-23T05:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a very minor eruption seen at approx. S30E80 in SDO AIA 304 beginning at approx. 2026-02-23T04:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

note: This CME is visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-23T13:38Z. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures visible on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

note: This CME is visible to the west/southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-23T13:38Z. The source may be related to an eruption observed on the southwest limb starting around 2026-02-23T22:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 171/304 and GOES SUVI 284/304 imagery. A subsequent faint opening of field lines is observed over the southwest limb in SDO AIA 171 imagery at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

note: This CME is visible to the north/northeast in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-02-23T13:38Z. Additionally, this event is not seen in SOHO LASCO C3 in real-time due to a planned gap between downlink periods starting at 2026-02-24T00:24Z. The source is an eruption centered near N30E30 starting around 2026-02-23T23:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. Dimming is observed north of the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 imagery, suggesting possible northern deflection, and post-eruptive arcades begin to form around 2026-02-24T00:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

note: CME faintly visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO A COR2. The potential source may be an eruption seen beyond the northwest limb as moving field lines starting around 2026-02-23T23:55Z in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

note: CME seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and very faintly to the SE in STEREO A COR2. The potential source is a slow, gradual eruption with dimming seen starting as early as 2026-02-23T23:12Z in SDO AIA 193 and 211 to the SE, spanning approximately S30E60 to S35E40.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.

The source eruption is likely beyond the southeast limb as seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery with additional moving field lines and post-eruptive arcades seen starting at 2026-02-24T14:51Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.

This CME is very faint in white light imagery and is easier to see in running difference imagery. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source of this CME is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of source candidate.

note: Extremely faint, slow CME with a fuzzy front seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2. It is likely too faint to see in GOES CCOR-1 or C3. It might be seen to the SSE in STEREO A COR2 in the first difference image after night time data gap but this is unreliable.  No clear source has been found for this CME, so it is likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the data gap in STEREO A coronagraph and no source has been found for it on the Earth-facing disk, so only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

note: Faint CME seen only in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph to the E. It is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Its source is likely the C2.6 flare peaking at 2026-02-25T06:56Z (S08W27), with an associated minor-size eruption seen in SDO AIA 193/304 as brightening/dimming and post-eruptive arcades. There is also a type II radio emission associated with this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary approximate one-coronagraph (STEREO A COR2) analysis based on the location of the likely source (C2.6 flare and eruption). Awaiting for backfill of SOHO LASCO data to triangulate.

note: Slow CME, possibly with streamer blowout and with diffuse front seen best to the NE in STEREO A COR2 and N in SOHO LASCO C2 (followed by multiple fronts). This CME is also seen in several early timestamps of GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3. Its source could be a minor filament eruption faintly seen moving in SDO AIA 304 and also indicated by a faint elongated dimming centered around N17W05 in SDO AIA 193, stretching N-S starting around 2026-02-25T06:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Analysis based on the fit in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). If the suggested faint filament eruption is the source of this CME, there is a significant deflection northward.

note: Very faint, slow and narrow CME loop seen to the SSE in C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and not seen in STEREO A COR2. No source has been found for it in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in available STEREO A COR2 imagery and no source has been found for it in EUV imagery. Only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

note: Very faint, narrow CME best seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2, seen faintly in a few early timestamps of C3 and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph, and not seen in STEREO A COR2. No source for it has been found on the Earth-facing disk, however there is an opening of field lines/restructuring of magnetic field behind the WSW limb as seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery after 2026-02-25T10:25Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative analysis based on the fit of a very minor front in STEREO A coronagraph with the more clear front in SOHO LASCO. No source found for this CME to confirm this analysis.

note: CME seen to the W in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). In STEREO A EUV 304/195 it is seen in the SW, close to the limb (S22W50), just to the E of the coronal hole. So the source location appears to be S22W102, as seen from Earth.

cmeAnalyses.note: Analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1); shows minor deflections eastwards (which is likely due to the coronal hole to the west of the erupting filament).

Coronal signatures include opening of field lines behind the limb seen in SDO AIA 193/171 and GOES SUVI 195/284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is too fuzzy to consistently track once it reaches the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3.

There is not an additional source signature on the Earth-facing disk. The leading edge accelerates later into the propagation into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement relying on SOHO LASCO C3 as the CME accelerates later in its propagation.

note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-02-25T20:24Z. It is also faintly seen for a couple frames in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is likely some faint field line movement from beyond the SE limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-02-25T19:20Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2.

The source of this event is field line movement seen along the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-02-26T12:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

The source is likely far-sided, although there is a possibility that a minor eruption centered near N55W05 in SDO AIA 193 imagery starting around 2026-02-27T17:24Z could have been associated with this event. This source is speculative as the time that the CME is observed nearly overlaps with the time the source is observed.

N40E20 with liftoff beginning at approx. 2026-02-28T19:48Z as seen in GOES SUVI 304. Dimming and field line opening seen in GOES SUVI 195 appears to suggest a strong Northern deflection of this CME.

SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

The source of this event is the eruption of a filament located at approx. N40E35 which was heavily deflected to the North as seen in GOES SUVI 304. This filament became unstable at approx. 2026-03-02T00:44Z and initially attempted to erupt, but most of the material fell back to the disk. This filament finally began to fully erupt at approx. 2026-03-02T08:44Z. Field line opening, minor dimming and post eruptive arcades are also visible in GOES SUVI 195 and 284 in association with this eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

N05W65 as seen in GOES SUVI 304, which began lifting off at approx. 2026-03-02T09:52Z. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 195 and 284 in association with this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

N50E20 following an earlier filament eruption and resulting CME: 2026-03-02T09:24Z, which this CME heavily overlaps.
